symptoms: Brown spots on leaves, fruit scarring, premature leaf drop
treatment: Apply fungicide (mancozeb, sulfur). Prune affected branches. Remove infected fruit. Apply weekly during wet seasons.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Improve air circulation. Remove fallen leaves in autumn. Avoid overhead watering.

symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, leaf curling, stunted growth
treatment: Spray with neem oil or sulfur-based fungicide. Reduce leaf wetness. Prune dense canopy to improve air circulation.
prevention: Ensure adequate spacing between trees. Regular pruning. Apply preventative spray in early season.

symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on lower leaves, yellowing above spots
treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicide. Stake plants for air circulation. Water at soil level only.
prevention: Mulch plants. Avoid overhead watering. Rotate crops yearly. Remove plant debris after season.

symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems, white fungal growth on leaf underside, fruit rot
treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil. Increase air circulation dramatically.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead watering. Improve air circulation. Sanitize tools between plants.

symptoms: Small circular lesions with dark borders and gray centers, black dots in center (pycnidia)
treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply fungicide (mancozeb, azoxystrobin). Avoid wetting foliage. Improve drainage.
prevention: Space plants properly. Mulch soil. Water at base only. Remove lower leaves as plant grows. Rotate crops.

symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves, brown vascular discoloration in stems
treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants. Sanitize soil. Plant resistant varieties (VF, FF). No effective fungicide.
prevention: Use resistant varieties. Avoid planting in contaminated soil. Rotate crops (3-4 years). Sanitize tools and equipment.

symptoms: Water-soaked spots on leaves, white fungal growth on leaf underside, stem rot, tuber decay
treatment: Remove affected plants. Apply mancozeb or copper fungicide. Improve air circulation. Increase spacing between plants.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead watering. Improve air circulation. Destroy affected tubers. Rotate crops.

symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on older leaves, yellowing and defoliation
treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply mancozeb or azoxystrobin. Improve air circulation. Prune lower foliage.
prevention: Remove lower leaves as plant grows. Mulch plants. Space adequately. Rotate crops. Destroy plant debris.

symptoms: Sudden wilting of foliage, yellowing, stem browning, potato tubers rot in storage
treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Do not compost. Control Colorado potato beetles (vector).
prevention: Plant certified seed potatoes. Control beetle vectors. Avoid overhead watering. Rotate crops (3 years minimum).

symptoms: Small angular brown spots with yellow halos, lesions on fruit, defoliation
treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicide. Avoid handling wet plants. Improve air circulation.
prevention: Use resistant varieties. Sanitize seeds (hot water treatment). Avoid overhead watering. Rotate crops annually.

symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems, fruit rot, stem girdling, wilting

prevention: Ensure excellent drainage. Avoid low-lying areas. Space plants properly. Use fungicide seed treatments. Rotate crops.

symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves and stems, leaf yellowing and drying
treatment: Spray with neem oil or sulfur-based fungicide. Prune affected areas. Improve air circulation.
prevention: Ensure adequate spacing. Avoid overhead watering. Apply preventative spray weekly. Remove infected leaves promptly.

prevention: Space plants properly. Avoid overhead watering. Apply preventative fungicide in humid conditions. Rotate crops.

symptoms: Reddish-brown pustules on leaves, yellow halo around lesions, leaf browning
treatment: Apply fungicide if severe (azoxystrobin or propiconazole). Remove infected leaves. Improve air circulation.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead watering. Remove corn debris after harvest. Practice crop rotation.

symptoms: Rectangular brown lesions with gray centers on lower leaves, yellow halos, leaf death

prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate crops (2-year minimum). Remove corn residue. Avoid overhead watering.

symptoms: Reddish-brown sunken lesions on pods and leaves, pink spore masses in wet conditions

prevention: Use resistant varieties. Use certified disease-free seeds. Avoid handling wet plants. Rotate crops (2 years).

symptoms: Mottling and mosaic patterns on leaves, leaf distortion, stunted growth, reduced yield

prevention: Control aphids with insecticide. Plant resistant varieties. Use disease-free seeds. Avoid hand contact. Rogue infected plants early.

symptoms: Small circular brown spots with dark borders on older leaves, gray center with black dots
